game_name: Wizardry II: The Knight of Diamonds

design_philosophy.core_loop: Party-based dungeon exploration with turn-based combat, character progression, and mandatory multi-level treasure collection quest
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Survival against escalating monster difficulty with resource management (health, spells, equipment) while searching for scattered armor pieces
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Leading a veteran adventuring party through a dangerous siege-driven scenario, proving mastery by recovering legendary Knight of Diamonds armor
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Explicitly designed as a challenging sequel requiring level 13+ characters from first game; enforces mandatory exploration of all six dungeon levels with no skip shortcuts; replaces convenience features with dungeon-saving to allow deeper, longer runs

difficulty.continues_system: Character death permanent unless resurrected; party wipe forces reload from dungeon save or restart
difficulty.checkpointing: In-dungeon saving introduced (improvement over first game which forced exit to town); allows players to preserve progress mid-exploration
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Pre-generated party option available in later versions; character creation allows class/build variety; difficulty floor set at level 13+ with 'baptism by fire' for underprepared parties

progression.upgrade_system: Character experience-based leveling system with spell power increases noted during progression; equipment acquisition from dungeon exploration
progression.unlock_mechanism: Six armor pieces scattered across six mandatory dungeon levels; collecting all pieces unlocks staff of Gnilda required to complete game narrative
replayability_hooks: Character import from first game creates narrative continuity
Multiple class/build options for new characters
Post-game dungeon exploration for additional challenges
Varied monster encounters per level creating distinct exploration experiences

world_building_techniques: Siege scenario framing (city under attack, rulers killed)
Quest-driven exploration (each level contains objective-based content)
Legendary artifact narrative (Knight of Diamonds armor as central MacGuffin)
Character continuity from prequel (importing veterans adds personal stakes)

faction_indicators: City of Llylgamyn under siege from external invaders
Party composition mirrors adventurer guild hierarchy
danger_signals: Rulers of Llylgamyn killed (loss of authority)
Level 13+ difficulty wall signals existential threat to unprepared adventurers
Mandatory deep dungeon exploration without safe retreat routes
environmental_storytelling: Six-level dungeon structure mirrors escalating siege peril
Each level contains quests/objectives indicating organized threats
Armor piece distribution implies progressive arming for final confrontation
